messages.content: i wanna save some struct variables with imgui::SaveIniSettingsToDisk  and nlohmann json library, show snippet how to do it

std::ofstream outFile("settings.json");
is marked as error

I apologize for the mistake. The `std::ofstream` line should be modified to create the file in binary mode, like this:

```c++
std::ofstream outFile("settings.json", std::ios::binary);
```

This will ensure that no newline translation is performed on the data when writing to the file. Please let me know if you have any further questions or issues.

why when i move / resize my imgui window it resets to previous location / size
i mean it resets when launched

I see! In that case, it's likely that your application is attempting to load the default "imgui.ini" file when it starts up, which is overwriting the settings that you saved previously. To prevent this from happening, you can either delete the default "imgui.ini" file before your application runs, or you can specify a custom INI file name using `ImGui::CreateContext()`.

1. Deleting the default "imgui.ini" file:

```c++
std::remove("imgui.ini");
ImGui::CreateContext();
```

This code deletes the default "imgui.ini" file before creating a new `ImGuiContext` instance. This ensures that there are no existing INI files to overwrite your window settings.

2. Using a custom INI file name:

```c++
ImGui::CreateContext(nullptr, "mysettings.ini");
```

This code creates a new `ImGuiContext` instance and specifies "mysettings.ini" as the INI file name. This will prevent ImGui from attempting to use the default "imgui.ini" file and ensures that your window settings are saved to a file with a custom name that you can choose.

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any further questions.
how can i stick buttons to bottom line of imgui window
buttonPos is not used anywhere
i need this button to stick on left bottom corner, not right bottom

No, the snippet you posted earlier will place the button on the bottom center of the window, as it is calculating the X coordinate using `windowSize.x/2 - 50` and the Y coordinate using `windowPos.y + windowSize.y - 40`.
